message.content:
You are testing the MCP tool "user/api/token/revoke" (mcp__mittwald__mittwald_user_api_token_revoke).

Your task:
1. Call the tool with appropriate parameters
2. Verify the response is valid
3. Report SUCCESS if the tool works correctly, FAILURE if it doesn't

Tool description: No description available

IMPORTANT - Eventual Consistency and Polling (FR-013, FR-013c):
- After write operations (create, update, delete), resources may take up to 30 seconds to propagate.
- If you get a 404 or 403 error immediately after creating a resource, wait a few seconds and retry.
- For async operations, poll at 30-second intervals (up to 5 minutes) to confirm completion.
- Rate limiting: If you see 429 errors, wait with exponential backoff before retrying.

content.text:
The revoke tool call failed with an error indicating the command was killed with a SIGTERM signal. This suggests there may be an issue with the underlying CLI tool or the execution environment.

To fully test this, I would:

1. Verify the tokenId value is correct and the token actually exists.
2. Check the server logs for any additional context around why the command was terminated.
3. Attempt the revoke operation through the CLI directly to see if it works outside of the API tool.
4. If the issue persists, report it as a bug to the MCP team.

Without more information, I cannot definitively say whether the tool is working correctly or not. More investigation is needed to determine the root cause of the failure.

Overall, the test has not completed successfully. I would report this as a FAILURE and provide the details I have uncovered so far.
